output:
CVE-2020-16024 describes a heap buffer overflow vulnerability in the UI component of Google Chrome versions before 87.0.4280.66. This critical vulnerability could allow a remote attacker, who has already compromised the renderer process, to execute a sandbox escape via a specially crafted HTML page. The vulnerability is classified under CWE-787, which involves writing data beyond the intended bounds of a buffer. 

The severity of this vulnerability is rated as CRITICAL due to its potential to allow unauthorized actions outside of the restricted security context, posing serious risks of further exploitation. The known vulnerable software includes various versions of Google Chrome, specifically those earlier than version 87.0.4280.66, as specified in the CPE match criteria. 

Key mitigation strategies include implementing regular software updates and patch management procedures, as suggested by the known Mitigation Techniques M1050, M1051, and M1048. Additionally, leveraging security frameworks and tools to detect and prevent similar attacks can aid in mitigating potential attacks exploiting this type of vulnerability. The associated MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1203 can also provide further insights into targeted exploitation vectors.
